raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
March 2026 closed with total spend of £20,928 against a budget of £15,465, a net overage of +£5,463 (+35.3%). The full variance is concentrated in two lines — Sales and Purchases — both of which are directly linked to a single driver: the Meridian Group contract, originally scheduled to close on 15 April 2026, was pulled forward by 22 days at the customer's request to align with their financial year-end. The early close triggered £5,003 of revenue recognition in March (first-month recurring plus accelerated annual components), exceeding the Sales budget by 83.4%. The associated onboarding deployment materials of £1,250 were procured in the same month under an approved purchase order, driving the Purchases overage of +£750 (+150.0%). All remaining line items carry immaterial variances and are listed in the Insignificant Variances section below.

LINE COMMENTARY

Sales | +£5,003 (+83.4%) | Budget: £6,000 | Actual: £11,003
The £5,003 favourable variance is driven entirely by the Meridian Group contract closing on 24 March 2026, 22 days ahead of its originally planned 15 April close date. The pull-forward was initiated at the customer's request to fall within their financial year-end. Revenue recognised in March comprises two components: a first-month recurring charge of £2,000 and an accelerated annual component of £3,003, totalling £5,003 — matching the variance precisely. The signed MSA was received from Meridian's legal team on 24 March and countersigned by the CFO on 25 March. As this represents a timing pull-forward rather than a new win above plan, April's pipeline will likely reflect the absence of this deal's recurring contribution unless the budget is adjusted accordingly — this requires confirmation with the Finance team.
Sources
- CRM - 2024-11-05T26:15:00Z - crm-sales-broad - crm-opp-2026-MER-001: Meridian Group — Stage: Closed Won. Close date moved from 15 April to 24 March. ARR: £4,800. One-time onboarding: £1,200. Pulled forward by 22 days.
- Gmail - 2024-11-05T18:15:00Z - gmail-sales-broad - gmail-2026-03-24-meridian: RE: Meridian Group — Contract execution. Signed MSA received from Meridian legal on 24 March. Revenue recognition memo attached. CFO countersigned 25 March.
- Slack - 2024-11-05T12:15:00Z - slack-sales-broad - sales-slack-2026-03-24: @channel Q1 close update — Meridian contract signed today, ahead of April target. Deal pulled forward at customer's request to beat their FY end. Total contract value £5,100.
- CRM - 2024-11-05T26:45:00Z - crm-sales-narrow - crm-opp-2026-MER-001: Revenue split — recurring £800/mo + one-time £1,200. Total March recognition: £2,000 first month + £3,003 accelerated annual. See revenue recognition memo.

Purchases | +£750 (+150.0%) | Budget: £500 | Actual: £1,250
The £750 adverse variance reflects deployment materials procured for the Meridian Group onboarding in March. A stock order of £750 for initial deployment was raised on 26 March via the standard procurement channel. The full actual of £1,250 is covered by purchase order PO-2026-0312, approved by the Operations Director on 26 March. A COGS reconciliation email confirms the purchase was expected given the in-month revenue outperformance on the Meridian deal. The overspend against the £500 budget is consistent with an onboarding not originally planned for March.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-09T12:15:00Z - slack-purchases-broad - ops-slack-2026-03-26: Stock order for Meridian onboarding raised — £750 materials for initial deployment. Standard procurement channel, PO attached.
- Gmail - 2024-11-09T18:15:00Z - gmail-purchases-broad - gmail-2026-03-26-cogs: COGS reconciliation for March — direct materials for Meridian onboarding posted in month. Variance is expected given revenue outperformance.
- Gmail - 2024-11-09T18:45:00Z - gmail-purchases-narrow - gmail-2026-03-26-po: Purchase order PO-2026-0312 approved by Operations Director — £1,250 for deployment materials.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following lines each carry variances below the significance threshold and require no further commentary at this time:
- Advertising & Marketing | +£45 (+0.6%)
- Audit & Accountancy Fees | -£3 (-6.1%)
- Bank Fees | -£30 (-100.0%)
- Cleaning | -£100 (-100.0%)
- Entertainment - 0% | -£50 (-100.0%)
- Entertainment - 100% Business | +£18 (+0.0%)
- General Expenses | +£124 (+103.6%)
- Light Power Heating | +£13 (+14.9%)
- Motor Vehicle Expenses | -£6 (-4.8%)
- Postage Freight Courier | -£50 (-100.0%)
- Printing Stationery | +£46 (+227.9%)
- Rent | -£46 (-4.4%)
- Repairs Maintenance | -£100 (-100.0%)
- Subscriptions | £0 (+0.0%)
- Telephone Internet | -£2 (-4.6%)
- Travel National | -£150 (-100.0%)
